core.action: First resolve V0 to an actual contract symbol via futures_display_main_sina before making historical queries; replace the 22220101 far-future default with the actual latest trading date

consequence.description: V0 is a placeholder representing 'latest main contract' that requires a discovery step; queries with V0 and 22220101 default may return stale or incorrect data instead of actual historical prices

core.when: When implementing corporate action adjustment factor handling for historical price data
core.action: Apply forward-fill (ffill) logic for adjustment factor gaps: use last known factor value until the next factor change date; gaps exceeding 1 day must still use the last known factor

consequence.description: Without forward-fill, adjustment factors become NaN during gap periods, causing historical prices to be unadjusted and misrepresenting true returns

core.when: When implementing JavaScript decryption for Sina stock data
core.action: Use py_mini_racer (V8 engine) for server-side JavaScript decryption — do not replace with Selenium or other browser automation tools

consequence.description: Replacing py_mini_racer with Selenium requires a full browser installation, significantly increasing deployment complexity and resource consumption

core.when: When processing MAC fund timestamps for Chinese market data analysis
core.action: Convert millisecond timestamps from UTC to Asia/Shanghai timezone; verify offset correctness during DST transitions and holiday trading sessions

consequence.description: Ignoring timezone conversion or using wrong offset causes trading time analysis to be offset by hours, leading to incorrect pattern recognition and signal timing

core.when: When constructing CZCE (郑州商品交易所) futures receipt URLs
core.action: Implement version-aware URL construction based on date threshold 20251101: use .xls format for dates before 20251101, use .xlsx format for dates on or after 20251101

consequence.description: Using incorrect file format causes 404 errors, breaking historical futures receipt data retrieval for post-transition dates

core.when: When implementing Baidu Finance calendar data retrieval
core.action: Acquire valid secure cookie via _get_baidu_cookie before each session — implement retry logic with proper ConnectionError handling for network failures

consequence.description: Without cookie acquisition, Baidu Finance calendar requests return authentication failures, causing calendar data retrieval to fail silently

core.when: When parsing institution holdings quarter format from stock_fundamental data
core.action: Parse quarter format as string encoding: last digit indicates quarter (1-3) or annual report (4), e.g., '20191' = 2019 Q1, '20194' = 2019 annual; use string manipulation, not date parsing

consequence.description: Using date parsing on quarter strings like '20191' causes silent parsing errors or wrong quarter assignments, corrupting institution holdings time series analysis

core.when: When implementing convertible bond indicator calculations
core.action: Compute dual-low indicator using bond price and premium*100 formula — premium must be calculated as (conversion_price - stock_price) / stock_price, then multiplied by 100 for normalization

consequence.description: Incorrect premium calculation breaks the dual-low strategy signal, causing misidentification of undervalued convertible bonds and potentially executing trades on bonds that do not meet the dual-low criteria

core.when: When implementing realized volatility calculations using Yang-Zhang formula
core.action: Implement Yang-Zhang formula as RV² = Vo + k*Vc + (1-k)*Vrs where Vo=overnight variance, Vc=close-to-open variance, Vrs=open-to-close variance, and k varies with trading period n

consequence.description: Incorrect implementation of Yang-Zhang formula produces inaccurate realized volatility estimates, causing faulty risk assessments and suboptimal position sizing in volatility-sensitive strategies

validation_threshold: formula structure missing Vo, Vc, Vrs components OR k parameter not parameterized by n → FAIL

core.when: When calculating overnight volatility for realized variance estimation
core.action: Verify that overnight volatility uses sample variance on normalized open-to-open log returns (not range-based estimator), and document why this approach was chosen over alternatives like Garman-Klass

consequence.description: Using range-based overnight estimator without normalization introduces scale bias across securities with different price levels, systematically distorting overnight risk estimates for high-price vs low-price assets

core.when: When calculating Rogers-Satchell volatility proxy
core.action: Verify Rogers-Satchell estimator uses high-low-open-close log returns to capture full intraday range — do not apply when OHLC data is incomplete or unavailable; combine with other estimators for robustness

consequence.description: Applying Rogers-Satchell estimator with incomplete OHLC data produces invalid volatility measurements, leading to incorrect risk calculations and potentially dangerous overconfidence in strategy position limits

validation_threshold: missing any of OHLC fields when applying Rogers-Satchell → FAIL

core.when: When calculating commodity futures roll yield
core.action: Filter out financial options (IO, MO, HO) identified by exchange code or contract symbol prefix before including contracts in roll yield analysis — these must not be mixed with commodity futures

consequence.description: Mixing financial index options with commodity futures in roll yield calculation introduces asset class contamination, producing misleading commodity roll dynamics that cause systematic losses in commodity-specific strategies

core.when: When migrating or using THS financial data APIs
core.action: Verify response schema compatibility when using stock_financial_xxx_ths (old /new/ endpoints) vs stock_financial_xxx_new_ths (new /astockpc/ endpoints) — verify field mappings are correct for each API version

consequence.description: Using mismatched field mappings between old and new THS API versions causes silent data parsing failures where financial metrics are read from wrong schema positions, corrupting fundamental analysis results

core.when: When parsing CPCA fuel market data for EV vs ICE vehicle analysis
core.action: Validate category names at runtime (整体市场, 销量占比-PHEV-BEV, 销量占比-ICE-NEV) and implement fallback handling for policy-driven category changes that may rename BEV/PHEV/NEV classifications

consequence.description: Hardcoded CPCA category names break when regulatory EV classifications change, causing silent data gaps in market share tracking that distort EV adoption trend analysis

core.when: When analyzing SGE spot gold and silver price basis
core.action: Separate spot contracts (Au99.99) from deferred contracts (Au(T+D), Ag(T+D)) in analysis — do not mix spot and deferred settlement data when calculating price basis or delivery costs

consequence.description: Mixing spot and deferred contract prices in basis calculations introduces systematic pricing errors due to different delivery windows and settlement rules between spot and T+D contracts

core.when: When implementing numerical methods for volatility surface interpolation or implied volatility solving
core.action: Explicitly implement convergence_tolerance and max_iter parameters for each numerical solvers — set explicit defaults (e.g., tolerance=1e-8, max_iter=1000) and verify results are reproducible across runs

consequence.description: Numerical methods without explicit convergence parameters either run indefinitely or use implicit hidden defaults, producing non-reproducible results where the same input yields different outputs on different runs

validation_threshold: numerical method missing convergence_tolerance OR max_iter parameter → FAIL

core.when: When implementing Yang-Zhang volatility estimation in risk calculation
core.action: Implement the k weight formula exactly as specified: k = 0.34/(1.34+(n+1)/(n-1)) — do not simplify to constants like 0.34 or use alternative weighting schemes

consequence.description: Simplifying the k weight formula to a constant 0.34 disrupts the optimal overnight/intraday volatility weighting, causing Yang-Zhang estimates to lose their statistical efficiency and produce systematically biased risk measures

core.when: When implementing data parsing logic for numeric columns from web data sources
core.action: Use pd.to_numeric with errors='coerce' for type conversion — invalid values must become NaN, not raise exceptions

consequence.description: Removing errors='coerce' causes the parser to raise exceptions on malformed data like percentage signs, dashes, or HTML artifacts, crashing the entire data pipeline and preventing backtest execution

core.when: When calculating volatility estimates for cross-sectional portfolio analysis
core.action: Use logarithmic returns for volatility calculation to verify scale invariance across securities with different price bases — do not use simple percentage returns

consequence.description: Using simple percentage returns breaks volatility additivity across time periods, causing cross-sectional comparisons to be meaningless and leading to incorrect portfolio construction with systematic over/under-weighting

core.when: When processing Hong Kong stock financial data queries
core.action: Verify symbol format is numeric without market prefix (e.g., '00700' for HK) — HK stocks require different format than A-shares ('SH600600', 'SZ000001'); do not apply A-share format conventions to HK symbols

consequence.description: Applying A-share symbol format to HK stocks (e.g., 'SZ00700') causes API query failures or returns incorrect data, silently corrupting financial data and producing invalid backtest results

validation_threshold: hk_symbol contains 'SH' OR hk_symbol contains 'SZ' → FAIL

core.when: When implementing comparative momentum analysis across different market capitalization index segments
core.action: Use percentage change via pct_change() for normalized returns — do NOT substitute absolute price differences for cross-index comparison

consequence.description: Using absolute price changes instead of percentage returns breaks cross-index comparability since different indices have vastly different absolute price levels, causing momentum signals to be systematically distorted

core.when: When retrieving historical price data using stock_zh_a_sina, cal/rv.py, or bond_zh_cov.py functions
core.action: Explicitly specify the adjust parameter ('qfq', 'hfq', or '' ) rather than relying on defaults — verify the returned price adjustment matches your strategy requirements

consequence.description: Default adjust values vary across functions ('' in stock_zh_a_sina, 'hfq' in cal/rv.py, '' in bond_zh_cov.py), causing users to receive unexpected price adjustments that alter backtest returns and strategy signals

core.when: When retrieving minute-level data using stock_hist_em or index_zh_em functions
core.action: Explicitly specify the period parameter rather than relying on defaults — verify the retrieved time window matches your strategy requirements (stock_hist_em defaults to '5', index_zh_em defaults to '1')

consequence.description: Period defaults differ between stock_hist_em ('5') and index_zh_em ('1'), causing users to receive different minute windows without understanding why, leading to data mismatches in multi-asset backtests

core.when: When making HTTP requests across different akshare modules (pro/client.py, utils/request.py, multi_decrypt.py)
core.action: Be aware that timeout defaults vary (10s in pro/client.py, 15s in utils/request.py, 30s in multi_decrypt.py) — explicitly set timeout for critical requests and handle timeout exceptions

consequence.description: Inconsistent timeout defaults across modules create unpredictable behavior — a strategy working with one API may fail with another due to different timeout thresholds, causing intermittent request failures

core.when: When rounding CNY prices in data parsing or storage
core.action: Round prices to exactly 2 decimal places using proper rounding (not truncation); verify internal calculations use full floating-point precision before rounding

consequence.description: Floating-point noise in CNY prices causes subtle rounding errors that accumulate over thousands of trades, leading to backtest equity curves that do not match live trading settlement amounts

core.when: When calculating realized volatility for CNY stocks
core.action: Use the Yang-Zhang formula which combines overnight volatility, close-to-close volatility, and Rogers-Satchell volatility with adaptive k weighting coefficient; single-estimator methods (Garman-Glasser, Parkinson) must not be used as primary estimator

consequence.description: Single-estimator volatility methods have higher estimation error in the presence of intraday jumps and overnight gaps, causing systematic misestimation of risk metrics that leads to improper position sizing

validation_threshold: model_type not in ['YangZhang', 'YZ'] AND estimator != 'yang_zhang' → WARN

core.when: When aggregating intraday minute-level realized variance to daily frequency
core.action: Use mean grouping to aggregate intraday realized variance to daily frequency; sum aggregation must not be used as it causes double-counting of volatility across overlapping time intervals

consequence.description: Sum aggregation overcounts volatility contributions from overlapping intraday periods, producing artificially inflated daily volatility estimates that lead to excessive risk adjustment and under-trading

core.when: When preparing DataFrames for volatility_yz_rv or rv_from_stock_zh_a_hist_min_em functions
core.action: Validate that input DataFrames contain required columns ['open', 'high', 'low', 'close'] before calling volatility functions; add explicit column validation with clear error messages if columns are missing

consequence.description: Passing DataFrames with incorrect column names to volatility functions causes silent errors or cryptic tracebacks, making debugging time-consuming and potentially leading to incorrect volatility calculations without warning

core.when: When using API credentials for currency or market data sources
core.action: Enforce explicit API key validation in each credential-consuming modules (currency.py, pro/client.py); verify missing credentials raise clear errors at initialization rather than defaulting to empty strings that cause silent failures

consequence.description: Empty API keys default silently, causing data fetch failures that only appear at runtime with confusing error messages, leading to strategy execution failures at unpredictable moments

core.when: When fetching historical price data for volatility calculations
core.action: Use consistent adjustment parameter (adjust='hfq') across each historical price fetches (stock_zh_a_sina, stock_zh_a_hist, etc.) and volatility calculations; do not mix adjusted prices with unadjusted prices across data sources

consequence.description: Using unadjusted prices for volatility calculations while other parts of the system use hfq-adjusted prices creates price discontinuity, causing volatility models to produce systematically biased risk estimates that lead to incorrect position sizing

core.when: When configuring API credentials for market data access
core.action: Hardcode API tokens in source code files (cons.py) or singleton config objects; use environment variables or secure credential vaults instead with explicit validation at startup

consequence.description: Hardcoded API tokens committed to version control create security exposure, and combined with inconsistent validation, allow credentials to be leaked while the system provides no error feedback for missing or invalid credentials

core.when: When processing QHKC fund position data queries
core.action: Assume QHKC data covers each date ranges; the proprietary platform only maintains data within the bounded window 2016-10-10 to 2019-09-30, and using datetime.date type does not prevent querying outside bounds

consequence.description: Querying QHKC data outside the 2016-10-10 to 2019-09-30 window returns empty data silently rather than raising errors, causing backtests to proceed with missing data that produces false results

core.when: When querying QHKC fund position data
core.action: Validate that start_date and end_date parameters are within the 2016-10-10 to 2019-09-30 range using datetime.date comparisons before API calls; raise ValueError with clear message if date bounds are violated

consequence.description: Without date range validation, queries outside the QHKC data window return empty results silently, causing backtests to proceed with missing fund position data that distorts portfolio analysis and risk calculations

core.when: When refactoring data acquisition functions that handle pagination in akshare.economic.macro_china
core.action: Preserve the duplicate removal logic that deduplicates records spanning pagination boundaries — do not remove or bypass the deduplication step

consequence.description: Removing pagination deduplication causes double-counting of economic observations, inflating indicator values and producing incorrect statistical calculations that lead to flawed strategy decisions

core.when: When implementing warning suppression in any akshare function
core.action: Use warnings.filterwarnings in function scope — this applies globally and persists for the session. Instead, use warnings.catch_warnings context manager for scoped suppression

consequence.description: Using warnings.filterwarnings in function scope suppresses warnings globally, hiding legitimate warnings from other code and making debugging extremely difficult when issues arise in unrelated parts of the session

core.when: When implementing or refactoring exception handling in akshare
core.action: Refine the exception hierarchy to be too granular — the current coarse-grained hierarchy (AkshareException base with APIError child) intentionally enables broad exception catching; overly specific categories break backward compatibility for users catching exceptions

consequence.description: Making the exception hierarchy too granular breaks existing code that catches library errors with single exception types, causing unhandled exceptions and potential service disruptions

core.when: When processing data with errors='coerce' parameter in pandas operations across the framework
core.action: Implement explicit error logging with configurable threshold alerts for coercion rates — BD-006, BD-037, BD-067 use errors='coerce' which silently converts invalid values to NaN, and combined with BD-061 and BD-030 this creates cascading silent failures

consequence.description: Silent NaN propagation from errors='coerce' creates hidden data quality failures where malformed data becomes NaN, propagates silently, and causes analysis to proceed with gaps leading to incorrect conclusions that are never flagged

core.when: When using Baidu Migration API for migration flow analysis
core.action: Assume Baidu Migration API provides complete data — the API caps responses at 100 records per area-indicator combination, and silently returns partial results for smaller regions

consequence.description: Assuming top100 results represent complete data underestimates migration flows for small regions, causing analysis to conclude insufficient population movement when data is simply capped

core.when: When analyzing Bloomberg Billionaires Index time series data
core.action: Assume Bloomberg Billionaires historical data is continuously available — archived source only maintains snapshots for 2018, 2019, 2021, with gaps for 2020 and most of 2022+

consequence.description: Assuming continuous historical data exists when gaps exist causes time-series analysis to produce incomplete or misleading trends, leading to incorrect conclusions about billionaire wealth evolution

core.when: When querying movie box office data through ENData API
core.action: Respect API date constraints: daily queries only return previous day data, weekly queries only complete weeks, monthly queries only complete months — do not attempt to query incomplete periods

consequence.description: Querying incomplete periods returns empty results or errors, causing incomplete box office analysis and missing revenue data for partial periods

core.when: When querying CCTV news data for historical sentiment analysis
core.action: Assume CCTV news data is available before 20160203 — the source only archives content from February 3, 2016 onward, and queries for earlier dates return empty results silently

consequence.description: Assuming CCTV data availability for pre-20160203 periods causes historical sentiment analysis to produce incomplete datasets with no indication of missing coverage

core.when: When performing historical news sentiment analysis on individual stocks
core.action: Assume East Money stock news provides complete historical coverage — the API returns only the 100 most recent news items per symbol, and historical news beyond 100 items is inaccessible

consequence.description: Assuming complete historical news coverage when only 100 recent items are available causes historical sentiment analysis to miss significant events, leading to incomplete understanding of stock behavior

core.when: When using QHKC tools for foreign ratios and GDP trend analysis
core.action: Assume QHKC tools provide historical data queries — the external ratio and GDP comparison tools are real-time only with no historical query capability documented as '暂不能查询历史数据'

consequence.description: Assuming historical data is queryable when tools are real-time-only causes trend analysis to return empty results with no indication that historical queries are unsupported

core.when: When calibrating quantitative models in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework computes calibration residuals or convergence diagnostics — the framework does not implement model calibration diagnostics; residuals and convergence checks are not available

consequence.description: Without calibration residuals and convergence diagnostics, underfitted or divergent models go undetected until production deployment, causing incorrect trading decisions and financial losses

core.when: When implementing model calibration in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement residual computation after each model fit by calculating predicted minus actual values, checking residuals against convergence thresholds, and logging diagnostic statistics (e.g., sum of squared residuals, R-squared)

consequence.description: Without calibration diagnostics, underfitted models are deployed to production without warning, leading to systematic prediction errors that accumulate over time and cause significant trading losses

core.when: When pricing derivatives or optimizing portfolios in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework validates no-arbitrage constraints in pricing models or portfolio optimization — the framework does not implement arbitrage constraint verification; each constraints are assumed satisfied without checking

consequence.description: Without no-arbitrage verification, arbitrage opportunities go undetected, causing the backtest to report phantom profits that cannot be realized in live trading, leading to significant capital losses when strategies fail

core.when: When implementing derivative pricing or portfolio optimization in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement arbitrage constraint verification by checking: (1) call-put parity bounds for options pricing, (2) non-negative weights in portfolio optimization, (3) transition matrix row sums in regime models; alert and halt on constraint violation

consequence.description: Without no-arbitrage constraint verification, strategies with embedded arbitrage are deployed to production, generating apparent profits that disappear upon live execution and causing substantial trading losses

core.when: When estimating covariance matrices for portfolio construction in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework provides covariance estimation method selection or shrinkage options — the framework uses a single default covariance estimator without user configurability; users cannot switch methods or apply regularization

consequence.description: Without covariance estimation configurability, the default estimator may be poorly suited to the portfolio size, causing ill-conditioned matrices that produce extreme portfolio weights and suboptimal allocations in live trading

core.when: When implementing covariance estimation for portfolio construction in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement covariance estimator selection with at minimum: Ledoit-Wolf shrinkage estimator as default option, Oracle Approximating Shrinkage (OAS), and Sample Covariance; document selected method in backtest reports for reproducibility

consequence.description: Without covariance estimator selection and shrinkage options, ill-conditioned covariance matrices produce portfolios with extreme weights, causing significant losses when rebalancing or during market stress events

core.when: When computing VaR or CVaR risk metrics in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework exposes VaR/CVaR confidence level or rolling window parameters — the framework uses fixed default values for confidence levels and lookback windows; these are not configurable by users

consequence.description: Without VaR/CVaR parameter configurability, risk metrics are computed with fixed assumptions that may not match the strategy's risk profile, leading to underestimation or overestimation of tail risk in live trading

core.when: When implementing VaR or CVaR risk calculations in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement VaR/CVaR parameterization with configurable confidence_level (e.g., 95%, 99%) and rolling_window (e.g., 252 for 1-year); require explicit parameter specification and document selected values in risk reports

consequence.description: Without configurable VaR/CVaR parameters, risk estimates are misaligned with actual strategy risk tolerance, causing inadequate capital reserves or excessive risk-taking that leads to significant losses during market drawdowns

core.when: When implementing portfolio rebalancing logic in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework provides configurable rebalancing trigger mechanisms — the framework has no rebalancing trigger controls; rebalancing logic is implicit and may use undisclosed fixed-interval defaults

consequence.description: Without rebalancing trigger mechanism configurability, the framework may use inappropriate rebalancing intervals, causing excessive trading costs or insufficient rebalancing that leads to tracking error and performance deviation

core.when: When implementing portfolio rebalancing in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement configurable rebalancing trigger mechanisms including: calendar-based (monthly/quarterly), threshold-based (drift percentage), or hybrid triggers; document trigger type and threshold values in backtest reports

consequence.description: Without configurable rebalancing triggers, fixed-interval rebalancing may cause excessive transaction costs in low-volatility markets or insufficient rebalancing in high-volatility markets, degrading portfolio performance

core.when: When computing carbon emissions in ESG/sustainability backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework tracks emission factor versions or data sources — the framework does not implement emission factor versioning; emission factors are used without version control or source documentation

consequence.description: Without emission factor versioning, changes to emission data sources are undetected, causing inconsistent carbon calculations across backtests and potential compliance violations in sustainability reporting

core.when: When implementing carbon emission calculations in ESG/sustainability backtesting
core.action: Implement emission factor versioning by storing factor versions with each calculation, recording data source identifiers, and logging emission factor metadata (e.g., supplier, publication date, regional scope) in backtest audit trails

consequence.description: Without emission factor versioning, sustainability reports use inconsistent data across time periods, causing compliance failures with ESG disclosure requirements and reputational damage from data provenance gaps

core.when: When computing ESG sustainability scores or carbon accounting in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework implements PCAF data quality scoring — the framework does not calculate or expose PCAF data quality indicators; data quality scoring is not available for emission or sustainability data

consequence.description: Without PCAF data quality scoring, emission calculations may use low-quality data without warning, causing non-compliant sustainability reports and potential regulatory penalties under GHG accounting standards

core.when: When implementing ESG or carbon accounting calculations in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement PCAF data quality scoring by calculating data quality scores per emission factor (0-100 scale based on source reliability, temporal relevance, geographic coverage), exposing scores in output reports, and flagging low-quality data sources

consequence.description: Without PCAF data quality scoring, backtest reports contain unverified emission estimates, causing non-compliant ESG disclosures that fail audit reviews and expose the organization to regulatory sanctions

core.when: When implementing data persistence or state management in the backtesting framework
core.action: Assume the framework provides versioned writes or snapshot semantics — the framework writes data without version control; snapshots do not preserve consistent state and overwrites cannot be audited or reversed

consequence.description: Without versioned writes and snapshot semantics, data overwrites are permanent and unauditable, causing irreversible data corruption that makes backtest results irreproducible and audit trails incomplete

core.when: When implementing data persistence and state management in the backtesting framework
core.action: Implement versioned writes by assigning sequential version IDs to each write operation, storing immutable snapshots with timestamps and metadata, and maintaining an audit log of each data modifications with user and timestamp attribution

consequence.description: Without versioned writes and snapshot semantics, accidental data overwrites corrupt backtest history, making results unreproducible and preventing error recovery without complete backtest reruns that waste computation time

core.when: When training reinforcement learning agents for trading strategies
core.action: Assume the RL reward function accounts for transaction costs — the framework does not model trading costs in RL reward functions; agents are trained without cost awareness and optimize for gross returns only

consequence.description: Without transaction cost modeling in RL rewards, trained agents generate excessive trading that appears profitable in backtests but becomes unprofitable in live trading due to real transaction costs eroding all gains

core.when: When implementing reinforcement learning reward functions for trading strategies
core.action: Implement transaction cost modeling in RL reward functions by adding cost parameters (commission_rate, slippage_bps, spread_cost), subtracting total transaction costs from each state-action reward, and documenting cost assumptions in strategy documentation

consequence.description: Without transaction cost modeling in RL rewards, trained agents develop high-frequency strategies that generate excessive trades, appearing profitable in backtests but losing money in live trading after transaction costs are applied

core.when: When using covariates in time-series backtesting or ML feature engineering
core.action: Assume the framework handles covariate time availability constraints — the framework does not implement time-aware covariate filtering; future covariate values are available in backtests and cause look-ahead bias

consequence.description: Without covariate time availability constraints, backtests use future covariate data that would not be available at prediction time, causing overly optimistic performance estimates that fail dramatically in live trading

core.when: When implementing covariate features in time-series backtesting or ML feature engineering
core.action: Implement time-aware covariate filtering by joining covariates only where covariate_timestamp <= target_timestamp, explicitly filtering unavailable future covariates, and adding temporal availability checks to prevent look-ahead bias

consequence.description: Without covariate time availability constraints, backtest equity curves exhibit look-ahead bias from future covariate access, causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results and leading to strategy abandonment

core.when: When building multi-agent trading systems using this framework for data fetching
core.action: Assume the framework provides multi-agent decision consensus mechanisms or risk veto rights — AKShare is a data retrieval library, not an AI/ML decision system; it does not implement consensus protocols or risk veto logic

consequence.description: Without consensus or veto mechanisms, multiple trading agents may simultaneously issue conflicting orders based on stale or inconsistent data fetched from different sources, causing race conditions and duplicate positions

core.when: When implementing multi-agent trading strategies that require consensus or risk veto
core.action: Implement a separate consensus layer (e.g., voting, priority-based arbitration, or quorum mechanisms) and risk veto logic outside AKShare; use AKShare only for data retrieval and implement each decision validation and veto checks in the application layer

consequence.description: Skipping consensus/veto implementation causes conflicting agent decisions to propagate directly to broker execution, potentially creating unauthorized positions or margin calls from simultaneous opposing trades

core.when: When calculating returns or comparing historical stock prices across corporate actions
core.action: Apply back-adjustment (hfq) for stock splits and dividends to verify price continuity — use adjusted prices rather than raw nominal prices for return calculations

consequence.description: Using unadjusted prices causes artificial volatility spikes at split/dividend dates, distorting return series and making volatility comparisons meaningless across corporate action events

core.when: When fetching historical economic data via paginated API with result size limits
core.action: Use day-by-day decrement pagination to verify complete data retrieval — do not use weekly or monthly date jumps that may skip data points in release schedules

consequence.description: Using weekly or monthly pagination jumps causes missing daily data points in economic time series, leading to gaps that corrupt historical analysis and policy/trading signal accuracy

core.when: When analyzing momentum or regime changes in economic indicators
core.action: Compute change rates across multiple horizons (1D, 3M, 6M, 1Y, 2Y, 3Y) — do not reduce to single timeframe as different horizons reveal distinct economic dynamics

consequence.description: Using only single timeframe loses ability to detect both short-term sentiment shifts and long-term trend changes, causing regime detection failures and suboptimal factor-based trading signals

core.when: When ingesting economic data with potential missing or invalid values
core.action: Convert invalid/missing values to NaN using errors='coerce' — do not raise exceptions as economic data legitimately has gaps during holidays and revisions

consequence.description: Using errors='raise' causes the entire data pipeline to fail on legitimate missing data during holidays or revisions, preventing macro analysis and trading signal generation

core.when: When computing growth rates for economic indicators to analyze economic cycles
core.action: Calculate both year-over-year (BASE_SAME/YoY) for seasonal trend and quarter-over-quarter (BASE_SEQUENTIAL/QoQ) for sequential momentum — both metrics required for complete cycle analysis

consequence.description: Using only YoY misses sequential momentum signals; using only QoQ misses seasonal trend patterns; incomplete analysis leads to wrong policy signals and trading decisions

core.when: When implementing Xueqiu data fetching functionality in backtesting pipelines
core.action: Bypass Xueqiu token authentication or use placeholder/null tokens for automation — valid authentication tokens are required; calling functions without proper tokens raises ValueError

consequence.description: Bypassing token authentication causes ValueError exceptions during data fetch, breaking automated backtesting pipelines and requiring manual intervention to obtain valid tokens

core.when: When implementing PnL calculation logic in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically conserves PnL across transactions — the framework does not implement PnL conservation checks; net profit calculations may contain discrepancies not visible in individual trade records

consequence.description: Without PnL conservation verification, cumulative backtest returns may show phantom profits or losses that do not reflect actual account balances, causing significant strategy evaluation errors

core.when: When implementing and evaluating backtest results
core.action: Verify PnL conservation by reconciling sum of individual trade PnLs against total portfolio PnL — implement daily/periodic PnL audit checks that flag discrepancies exceeding 0.01% of gross PnL

consequence.description: Without PnL reconciliation, hidden bugs in trade execution or fee calculations cause backtest results to diverge from actual trading outcomes by amounts that compound over time

core.when: When implementing train/test data split logic in strategy development
core.action: Assume the framework automatically enforces temporal integrity in train/test splits — the framework does not implement time split validation; future data may leak into training sets if not manually verified

consequence.description: Without train/test split integrity checks, look-ahead bias causes strategies to appear profitable in backtesting but fail catastrophically in live trading due to data leakage

core.when: When implementing train/test split for strategy backtesting
core.action: Verify that train end_date < test start_date and enforce strict temporal ordering — implement split validation that raises error if any training observation timestamp >= any test observation timestamp

consequence.description: Data leakage between train and test sets inflates strategy performance metrics, leading to incorrect strategy selection and significant live trading losses

core.when: When implementing signal generation and order execution timing in bar-based backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically handles correct bar execution timing for signals — the framework does not implement signal-to-bar alignment validation; signals may execute in incorrect bar positions

consequence.description: Without proper bar execution timing, signal orders execute at wrong prices within the bar, causing backtest fills to deviate significantly from actual execution prices

core.when: When implementing signal generation and order placement in bar-based backtesting
core.action: Align signal timestamps with correct bar boundaries — verify signals generated at bar t execute using bar t+1 open/high/low/close prices, not current bar prices (close price execution implies look-ahead bias)

consequence.description: Mismatched signal-bar alignment causes look-ahead bias where orders fill at prices not yet available, making backtest results unrealistically optimistic compared to live trading

core.when: When implementing carry and funding cost calculations in backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework automatically models funding/carry costs — the framework does not implement carry cost calculations; overnight positions are treated as cost-free

consequence.description: Without carry cost modeling, strategies holding overnight positions appear more profitable than they actually are, leading to strategies that underperform when actual financing costs are applied

core.when: When implementing position holding logic for overnight trades in backtesting
core.action: Explicitly model carry/funding costs by calculating and deducting position_value * daily_funding_rate for each overnight holding period — document funding rate assumptions and verify against actual broker rates

consequence.description: Ignoring carry costs causes strategies with overnight positions to show inflated returns; in live trading, financing costs for leveraged or short positions significantly reduce profitability

core.when: When implementing backtesting logic for strategy validation
core.action: Assume the framework provides backtest overfitting protection — the framework does not implement walk-forward analysis, cross-validation, or out-of-sample testing mechanisms

consequence.description: Without overfitting protection, strategies optimized on historical data will appear profitable in backtests but fail in live trading, leading to systematic capital losses

core.when: When validating strategy robustness in backtesting
core.action: Implement walk-forward analysis with out-of-sample testing: split data into train window (e.g., 60%) and test window (e.g., 40%), validate performance on test set before deployment

consequence.description: Strategies without overfitting protection will be selected based on in-sample performance, causing live trading returns to fall far below backtested results

core.when: When processing market orders in backtesting order execution
core.action: Assume each market orders are fully filled — the framework does not implement partial fill simulation or volume-based fill probability

consequence.description: Assuming 100% fill for large market orders overestimates execution feasibility; in live trading, orders may be partially filled or rejected, causing backtest-live discrepancy

core.when: When simulating order fills for large-size market orders in backtesting
core.action: Implement volume-based fill simulation: compare order_size against average_daily_volume (ADV), apply fill_ratio = min(1, ADV * 0.1 / order_size) for large orders, reject orders exceeding liquidity threshold

consequence.description: Without liquidity-aware fill simulation, backtests assume full execution for large orders that would be partially filled in live markets, inflating backtest returns by 10-30% for illiquid stocks

core.when: When implementing position adjustment logic for A-share backtesting
core.action: Assume the framework defines sell-before-buy vs buy-before-sell execution order — the framework does not implement explicit execution order logic

consequence.description: Without explicit execution order, the framework may execute in ambiguous order causing position miscalculations; for A-share markets, sell-before-buy is required to avoid leverage

core.when: When implementing order execution logic for A-share backtesting
core.action: Implement explicit sell-before-buy execution order: process each sell orders first to release locked capital, then process buy orders — verify capital adequacy before buy execution

consequence.description: Executing buy orders before sell creates hidden leverage when capital is insufficient; in live A-share trading, buy orders may be rejected, causing execution failure and strategy breakdown

core.when: When interpolating volatility for option pricing across strikes and maturities
core.action: Assume the framework implements volatility surface interpolation — the framework does not provide built-in interpolation methods for constructing smooth volatility surfaces from discrete strike-expiry data

consequence.description: Without explicit interpolation, raw discrete volatility data causes pricing discontinuities and model instability across strike-expiry grid

core.when: When constructing volatility surfaces for option pricing models
core.action: Implement volatility surface interpolation: use SABR model or cubic spline interpolation across strikes (delta dimension) and calendar spread interpolation across maturities (time dimension)

consequence.description: Without smooth volatility interpolation, option prices exhibit discontinuities at strike boundaries, causing arbitrage opportunities and pricing errors of 2-5% for deep ITM options

core.when: When calculating implied volatility from market option prices
core.action: Assume the framework provides implied volatility solver — the framework does not implement numerical methods for solving Black-Scholes inverse problem to extract IV from market prices

consequence.description: Without IV solver, the system cannot calibrate volatility surfaces from market data or backtest volatility-based strategies, causing critical features to be non-functional

core.when: When calibrating volatility models or extracting IV from market option prices
core.action: Implement implied volatility solver using bisection method or Newton-Raphson: initialize IV bounds [0.01, 3.0], iterate until price_diff < 1e-6, verify convergence and handle non-convergence cases

consequence.description: Without IV solver, volatility surface calibration is impossible, making options pricing inaccurate and backtesting of volatility strategies yield meaningless results
